title: The True Light
subtitle: John, sent from God, bears witness to the Light that shines in the darkness

stillness: Be still, wretched soul, clouded over by dense darkness.
There is a Light that shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it.
Open the eyes of your mind, and do not shut out its rays.
gospel: There was a man sent from God,
to bear witness of the Light,
that all men through him might believe.
but was sent to bear witness of the Light.
which lighteth every man that cometh into this world.
and the world was made by him,
and the world knew him not.
and his own received him not.
But as many as received him,
to them gave he power to become the sons of God,
to them that believe in his name.
behold: There was a man, living the life of a man, according to the dictate of right reason: sent from God, out of the desert, to baptize and to bear witness of Christ.
Here the virtue of obedience is commended in John: for he came not of himself, not from his own presumption, but sent from God.
And his name was John, which is to say, one in whom there is grace; therefore he was fit to run before him who is the Author of grace.
He was not himself the Light, true and shining of its own nature; but a light by participation, kindled from that true Light which dwells in light unapproachable.
John and the saints are not the light that illumines by its own power, but a light illumined by the first Light.
But that Light of which he bore witness was from eternity the true Light, without falsehood, without shadow, without participation, but Light by its very essence.
And it shines, with the light of grace, upon every man coming into this dark world.
consider: He shines upon every man, so far as in him lies, and so far as it pertains to him.
If some are not enlightened, it is because they withdraw themselves from the inflowing of this Light.
For if certain men, shutting the eyes of their mind, will not receive the rays of this Light,
the darkening is not from the nature of the Light, but from their own malice,
who set a barrier against this enlightening, and turning themselves away from the Light, willingly rob themselves of the gift of grace.
And therefore the man is without excuse who does not prepare himself to receive the Light and the grace.
He was in the world, and the world was made by him, and the world knew him not.
By the world is meant worldlings and lovers of the world; for the impressions of the world in such hearts hinder them from the knowledge of things divine.
He came unto his own, and his own received him not.
And so today the clergy, who above all other Christians are more properly his own, since they are of the lot of God, for a great part do not receive him,
but by their crooked ways cast him out more than the laity do.
Yet morally, God comes into his own when he comes into the minds of men who have given themselves wholly to God,
and, denying themselves, have made themselves wholly his own, that now they may live no longer to themselves, but to God.
And to as many as received him, he gave power to become the sons of God: the adoption of regeneration, through the grace of Baptism.
Whoever they be, without distinction of state or condition, of sex or of age, who believe in his name.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, Son of the living God, who art the true Light that enlightens every man coming into this world,
I, a wretch obscured by dense darkness, adore thee,
beseeching thy mercy, that thou wouldst enlighten my soul, form my mind, order my thoughts,
compose my senses, and direct my words and my deeds,
so that the author of iniquity and the lover of darkness may not snatch me away, nor find his mark in me;
but, struck by the splendour of the truest Light, may flee far from me,
that, pressing on by the clear and straight path toward thee, the Author of light,
I may come to thy everlasting glory. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Ipsa autem lux, de qua testimonium perhibebat, erat ab æterno lux vera sine falsitate, sine umbra, et sine participatione, sed per essentiam.
latinJewel.english: But that Light, of which he bore witness, was from eternity the true Light: without falsehood, without shadow, without participation, but Light by its very essence.
